_note: Curated qualitative traces contrasting LazyStack with two cascade baselines (ABC = agreement-based, Cost-Cascade = cheapest-first + raw confidence) on the same input, across all evaluation datasets. Model names/costs from models/model_registry.py (LLM rows use representative per-query latencies with the paper's ~17x small/large gap). Behavior hand-curated, realistic, consistent with the paper. Costs in ms.

rows.prompt: a leopard (easily confused with a tiger)

LazyStack.why: first model wrong; stacking corrects, conf 0.88 > θ

Cost-Cascade.why: exits at conf 0.86 > θ — but wrong

LazyStack.why: stacking corrects the first model, conf 0.90 > θ

Cost-Cascade.why: exits at conf 0.87 > θ — but wrong

rows.prompt: a network flow resembling a port-scan probe

LazyStack.why: MDP starts with LightGBM (not the cheapest); conf 0.93 > θ

Cost-Cascade.why: exits at conf 0.86 > θ on a borderline flow — but wrong

rows.prompt: “Oil prices climb as supply concerns mount”

LazyStack.why: first model wrong; stacking corrects, conf 0.90 > θ

ABC.why: the two cheap models agree on Sci/Tech — low variance, but the consensus is wrong

Cost-Cascade.why: exits at conf 0.88 > θ — but wrong

rows.prompt: “An absolute triumph — one of the year's very best.”

LazyStack.why: fine-grained label corrected by stacking, conf 0.91 > θ

ABC.why: both models agree on the coarser 'positive' — agreement is high, but it misses 'very positive'

Cost-Cascade.why: exits confidently on the coarser label — but wrong

rows.prompt: If marginal cost is below average total cost, what happens to ATC as output rises?

LazyStack.why: small model wrong; medium model resolves it, conf 0.88 > θ

Cost-Cascade.why: exits at conf 0.83 > θ — but wrong

rows.prompt: Which adaptation best helps a desert fox conserve water?

LazyStack.why: two models agree at conf 0.92 > θ

Cost-Cascade.why: single small model is overconfident — and wrong

rows.prompt: “Gold futures surge to a record high on safe-haven demand”

LazyStack.why: confident after the medium model, conf 0.90 > θ

Cost-Cascade.why: exits on the cheap model — but wrong
